name: George Adams
abstract: George Rufus Adams (April 29, 1940 – November 14, 1992) was an American jazz musician who played tenor saxophone, flute and bass clarinet. He is best known for his work with Charles Mingus, Gil Evans, Roy Haynes and in the quartet he co-led with pianist Don Pullen, featuring bassist Cameron Brown and drummer Dannie Richmond.

about_quotes.speaker: Sheila Jordan
about_quotes.evidence: George Russell... recorded it for Riverside... George got that record date... George told me... George's idea... I don't think I would ever have been heard had it not been for George Russell.
about_quotes.doc_title: Sheila Jordan — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Roy Haynes
about_quotes.evidence: George Adams was one of the famous guys that come out of there... Adams was with me then.
about_quotes.doc_title: Roy Haynes — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Pete Levin
about_quotes.evidence: There was one European tour we did, the band was a little bigger than usual but the sax section was Dave Sanborn, Arthur Blythe, Steve Lacy and Howard Johnson and George Adams.
about_quotes.doc_title: Pete Levin interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Mingus
about_quotes.evidence: There's a guy came to the club, wanted to see my man on tenor saxophone... I might have if I didn't have George Adams there at the same time.
